title: Email attachment phantom-success bug identified — gog gmail send --attach silently drops missing files
symptom: gog gmail send --attach returned a message_id (appeared successful) but email arrived with no attachment. User confirmed no file received. File had also been generated ephemerally and did not survive session.
root_cause: Two compounding issues: (1) gog gmail send --attach does not validate file existence before reporting success — it returns a message_id even if the attachment path is invalid or missing. (2) The .pptx file was generated in a session without durable commit to disk — file existed transiently and was cleaned up before send.
fix: Regenerated file using full generation script, verified with ls + file size check before send. Used gog with correct absolute path. Email delivered successfully.
lesson: NEVER trust gog gmail send --attach return code alone. Always verify: (1) file exists at path, (2) file size > 0 bytes, (3) follow up with gog gmail search to confirm email landed. Also: all generated deliverable files must be saved to a durable path immediately (~/Documents/ or Dropbox) not temp/session paths.
prevention: Add 3-step delivery verification to all file-send workflows: pre-check file, send, post-check inbox. Back up all generated files to Dropbox /Jarvis Reports/ as a separate step before email send.
